title: rosa gallica f. assimilis
intro: Rosa gallica f. assimilis, also known as the French rose or red rose, is a subspecies of the Gallic rose (Rosa gallica) that is native to Europe and Western Asia. This perennial shrub is valued for its fragrant, double flowers and has been used in herbal medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa gallica f. assimilis include its ability to reduce stress and anxiety, improve digestion, and support the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal teas and infusions. Rosa gallica f. assimilis can be prepared as a herbal tea by infusing the dried flowers in hot water, or as a tincture by steeping the flowers in a solvent such as ethanol or glycerin.
